ℹ️ Rows in the Dangerous tab are also present here. Use the Dangerous Only filter above to cross-reference. Object Type = All means the right applies to all object types / attributes.
⚠️ Review Required. The permissions below can be abused to compromise AD objects, escalate privileges, or steal credentials. Not all findings are exploitable — expected delegations (e.g. Helpdesk resetting passwords) will appear here. Verify each identity is intentionally granted these rights, is tightly scoped, and is documented. Investigate any unexpected or unfamiliar trustees immediately.

⚠ Dangerous Permissions

Loading…
OU / Distinguished Name Identity (Trustee) Rights Risk Level Reason & Description Type Object Type Inherited
Remediation — remove an ACE via PowerShell:
$acl = Get-Acl "AD:\OU=Name,DC=domain,DC=com"
$ace = $acl.Access | Where-Object { $_.IdentityReference -eq "DOMAIN\User" -and $_.ActiveDirectoryRights -match "GenericAll" }
$acl.RemoveAccessRule($ace)
Set-Acl -Path "AD:\OU=Name,DC=domain,DC=com" -AclObject $acl

Always document changes and verify in a test environment first. Consider using AD Delegation Wizard to re-apply standard delegations cleanly.
